linapro-oidc-discord.description: Provides general-purpose Discord third-party login (OAuth settings, optional auto-provisioning, and identity linking) for login entries, account binding, and other product flows. Requires Third-party Login - Core.
linapro-oidc-discord.name: Third-party Login - Discord

callback.error: Discord login failed. Please try again.
callback.unlinked: This Discord account is not linked to any local user. Please contact the administrator.

settings.title: Discord Login Settings
settings.description: Configure the Discord OAuth 2.0 client used for third-party login. The redirect URL must match the callback URL registered in the Discord Developer Portal.
settings.clientIdHelp: The client ID from the OAuth application you created in the Discord Developer Portal. Think of it as the app’s account name.
settings.clientIdPlaceholder: Enter the Discord OAuth 2.0 client ID
settings.clientSecretHelp: The secret paired with the client ID. Think of it as the app’s password. Keep it safe. Leave empty to keep the stored secret unchanged.
settings.clientSecretPlaceholder: Enter the Discord OAuth 2.0 client secret
settings.clientSecretKeepPlaceholder: Leave unchanged to keep the stored secret
settings.redirectUrlHelp: After users finish signing in with Discord, they return to this LinaPro address. It is generated automatically—copy it into the Redirects of your Discord application OAuth2 settings.
settings.redirectUrlHint: Fixed by the plugin. Copy this URL into the Redirects of your Discord application OAuth2 settings.
settings.saveFailed: Failed to save Discord login settings.
settings.loadFailed: Failed to load Discord login settings.
settings.defaultRedirectHelp: The page opened after a successful login into the workspace. Leave empty to use the system default home page. This is not the callback URL above. Ignored when SSO delivery matches a rule.
settings.defaultRedirectHint: Default page path after a successful third-party login into the workspace. Leave empty to use the system default home page. Does not change the Callback URL above; ignored when SSO delivery matches a rule.
settings.enableSsoHelp: For other business systems: they start login with an agreed key, and after success the tokens go straight to their receiver URL instead of the LinaPro workspace.
settings.enableSsoHint: When enabled, logins started with a matching state key deliver the tokens straight to the configured third-party receiver URL instead of the workspace.
settings.rulesHelp: Each rule is one business scenario: the left side is the key the other system sends when starting login; the right side is where tokens are delivered after success.
settings.ruleKeyHelp: The identifier (state key) the other system includes when starting login. Use it to tell scenarios apart, for example app-a or portal-b.
settings.ruleUrlHelp: Where to send the login tokens after success. Use the receive URL provided by the other system.
settings.copyFailed: Failed to copy the login URL.
settings.autoProvisionHelp: When on: a first-time Discord login that is not linked to a local account creates a least-privilege platform user. If the email already belongs to an existing account, login is rejected until that account links the identity.
settings.autoProvisionHint: When enabled, a verified Discord identity without a linked local account gets a least-privilege platform account automatically. If the email already belongs to an existing account, login is rejected until the identity is linked from that account.
